summary:
Klippel-Trenaunay Syndrome is associated with pulsatile varicose veins in the lower extremities, characterized by persistent embryonic vein remnants leading to venous stasis and chronic thrombosis.
The presence of such symptoms reliably indicates KLIPPEL-TRENAUNAY SYNDROME.
